findings.issue: Size declared M but feature touches 5 SKILL.md files plus 3 new reference files — not fixed from round 1
findings.why_matters: The spec modifies design-system-init, design-generate, design-review, design-retrospective, and code-writing (5 SKILL.md files), creates 3 new reference files (taste-profile template, designer-experience, style-profiles), and adds a cross-cutting two-layer description concern to all 4 design skills. The spec still declares size: M with no change since round 1. This is L-territory by the project's own sizing criteria (new architecture / multiple interconnected components with cross-cutting concerns).
findings.fix: Either re-declare size as L, or split into two independently deliverable increments: (1) taste-profile + designer-experience + retrospective writes (core learning loop), (2) style-profiles + two-layer descriptions + code-writing integration (enhancements). Each increment ships independently.

findings.issue: Scenario G edge cases partially fixed — 'user requests technical-only output' still missing
findings.why_matters: Round 1 flagged zero edge cases for Scenario G. The spec now covers: no technical justification available (human layer only), and design-review lightweight context (optional two-layer). The third case — user explicitly requesting technical-only descriptions — is still absent. This is a plausible user preference (developer audience) that would affect all 4 skills.
findings.fix: Add one edge case to Scenario G: 'User explicitly requests technical-only output → skip human-language layer, provide only technical details.' One sentence suffices.

findings.issue: Concurrent write risk on designer-experience.md mitigated but not fully resolved
findings.why_matters: Round 1 flagged concurrent writes to the shared global file. The spec now specifies append-only semantics with 'безопасно для последовательных проектов' (safe for sequential projects). Append-only significantly reduces data loss risk for typical sequential usage. However, the mitigation is explicitly scoped to sequential projects — simultaneous runs remain unaddressed. For an agent-driven markdown workflow this is low-probability, but the risk is acknowledged and partially unresolved.
findings.fix: Acceptable as-is for MVP given the low-probability of true simultaneous runs. Optionally add to risks section: 'Simultaneous writes to designer-experience.md may cause interleaved content; mitigation: append-only format reduces severity, full resolution deferred post-MVP.'

findings.issue: No observability for the learning loop — not fixed from round 1
findings.why_matters: Core value proposition is agent learning over 3+ sessions. If taste-profile.md accumulates incorrect preferences or experience categorization drifts, there is no way for the user to trace why the agent makes certain suggestions. No timestamps, no session provenance, no audit trail specified.
findings.fix: Add to acceptance criteria: taste-profile.md entries include session date/number as source. This allows user to trace and manually correct drift without reverse-engineering the file.

findings.issue: Scenario D (code-writing integration) remains in scope — not extracted
findings.why_matters: The code-writing tokens.json integration is a convenience feature orthogonal to the learning/memory theme (it applies existing tokens, not accumulated preferences). Round 1 flagged this as scope that could be deferred. It remains in scope unchanged, contributing to the L-sized reality of the feature.
findings.fix: Consider extracting Scenario D as a separate S-sized feature ticket. If kept, add a note in the spec that Scenario D can be deferred if core learning loop (Scenarios A-C) takes longer than estimated.

summary: Round 1 partially addressed: Scenario G now has 2 of 3 edge cases added (fixed), designer-experience.md has append-only semantics specified (partially fixed), but size M declaration is unchanged despite 5 SKILL.md files + 3 new reference files clearly placing this in L territory. No critical blockers — spec is buildable and coherent — but the sizing mismatch should be resolved before implementation to set correct expectations.
